location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in San Francisco office

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in product management
Ownership over major product surface (Agent Security & Governance)
Expected to shape product roadmap and team itself

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 8+ years of experience in product management at SaaS companies, primarily serving large enterprises with responsibilities spanning security, governance, trust, identity, data integrations.
must_haves.qualifier: primarily serving large enterprises with responsibilities spanning security, governance, trust, identity, data integrations

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have a strong technical background and experience with both UX and API driven products, integration paths, and 3rd party product partnerships.
You are an excellent written and verbal communicator.
You have a proven track record of taking ownership, taking initiative, and delivering results.
You collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners.
You have a learning and growth mindset.
You are mission-first and understand that your success is measured by your product and team's success.

scope.surface: Agent Security & Governance — explainable/transparent AI systems, AI risk identification and prevention, observability, security, and privacy features around AI agents and underlying data
primary_metrics_inferred: Enterprise customer trust and adoption of AI agents
core_responsibilities: Spending a lot of time with our customers to deeply understand their knowledge needs
Developing key parts of our product roadmap, marrying customers' needs with our product vision
Empowering your team by giving context, setting direction, and building alignment
Driving customer-focused decisions, clear prioritization, and efficient execution
Coordinating cross-functionally with leadership, go-to-market teams, and other key stakeholders across the company
Building processes that will scale as our team and company go through rapid growth

Developing new technologies and product features spanning explainable / transparent AI systems, identification and prevention of various vectors of AI risks, and the observability, security and privacy features around AI agents and underlying data

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as possible for certain roles; form not specified

green_flags: Clear and specific product surface area (Agent Security & Governance)
Company is well-recognized (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, CNBC Disruptor 50)
Lean product team signals high ownership and impact potential
Comprehensive benefits package including 401k, education, wellness, home office stipend
red_flags: Very wide compensation range ($160K-$280K) makes it difficult to assess target level without additional context
4 days/week in San Francisco office is near-onsite and eliminates most non-SF candidates
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD
No specific mention of team size or reporting structure
8+ year minimum is a high bar that may filter out strong mid-level candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and compensation range)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred false as no people management mentioned)

ambiguities: Compensation range of $160K-$280K is unusually wide; may reflect multiple seniority levels or locations, making target level ambiguous.

Nice-to-haves section is absent — all listed requirements appear in an 'About you' section with unconditional language, so all are treated as hard must-haves.
